The application is a transactional backend.
The application processes ATM cash withdrawal requests.
The application processes withdrawal requests securely.
The application includes a minimal administrative console.
The application enforces balance rules.
The application enforces limit rules.
The application flags suspicious activity.
The application guarantees that any failed transaction is fully rolled back.
The application authenticates users by card number before any transaction.
The application authenticates users by PIN before any transaction.
The application locks the account after 3 consecutive failed authentication attempts.
The application declines withdrawals exceeding the account balance.
The application declines withdrawals exceeding the daily limit per calendar day.
The application processes each withdrawal atomically.
The application rolls back all steps completely if any step fails.
The application ensures the balance is unchanged after a failed withdrawal.
The application detects suspicious withdrawal patterns.
The application flags transactions for administrator review.
The application lets administrators review flagged transactions.
The application lets administrators lock accounts.
The application lets administrators unlock accounts.
The application lets administrators view transaction histories.
The application records every authentication attempt with a timestamp.
The application records every transaction state change with a timestamp.
The system verifies a user's identity through a combination of a card number and a personal identification code.
It confirms the credentials provided by the cardholder before allowing any cash withdrawal request to proceed.
Customers must correctly enter their bank card number and secret PIN to start a transaction.
Successful authentication grants access to proceed with a withdrawal; failure blocks further steps.
Identity validation is performed using a plastic card identifier and a confidential numeric password.
The security mechanism disables further login attempts after three consecutive incorrect PIN entries.
After three successive authentication failures, the system permanently blocks access for that card.
If a user enters the wrong PIN three times in a row, they will be unable to attempt further logins.
The cardholder's account becomes inaccessible for any transaction until an administrator reactivates it.
Lockdown of an account occurs automatically following a trio of unsuccessful credential submissions.
The system enforces rules that prevent withdrawals exceeding the available funds or the predefined maximum per calendar day.
It compares the requested amount against the account's current balance and the remaining daily allowance, and declines if either limit is exceeded.
Users cannot request cash amounts that go beyond their current balance or the daily withdrawal cap.
Any withdrawal attempt that surpasses either limit is rejected, preserving the account from overdraft or limit violation.
Financial constraints are applied by verifying available credit and a per-day ceiling for cash removal.
Each withdrawal is processed as an all-or-nothing operation; if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.
The system performs the entire withdrawal sequence in a single unit, and upon any error, automatically reverses every modification made.
The user experiences either a completely successful cash dispensation or a full reversal with no net effect on their balance.
The account balance remains unchanged if the transaction fails, ensuring no partial deductions occur.
Transactions are executed indivisibly, and any failure triggers a total reversion to the original state.
The system analyzes withdrawal behavior for anomalies and marks transactions that appear potentially fraudulent for later inspection.
It monitors transaction patterns in real time and assigns a flag to any activity that matches predefined suspicious criteria.
Users are unaware of the detection happening behind the scenes; only flagged items are brought to administrative attention.
Suspicious withdrawals are tagged in the system, alerting administrators to review them for possible fraud.
Unusual cash request patterns are identified and labelled for investigative follow-up.
Authorized personnel can examine transactions that have been marked as suspicious to decide on further action.
The administrative console provides a list of flagged items, enabling operators to inspect details and take appropriate measures.
Admins access the flagged transaction queue to review each case and decide whether to approve, block, or investigate further.
Flagged transactions are investigated and either cleared or escalated, maintaining system integrity.
Designated managers scrutinize marked cash movements to evaluate their legitimacy.
Administrators possess the ability to manually enable or disable a cardholder's access to the system.
Through the admin interface, authorized users can toggle the locked status of any account instantly.
When an administrator locks an account, the cardholder cannot perform any transactions until unlocked.
The account's operational state changes immediately, either blocking all activity or restoring full capabilities.
Authorized operators can freeze or unfreeze a customer's access at will.
The system maintains a chronological record of every authentication event and transaction state change, each annotated with the exact time.
It automatically logs each login attempt and any modification to transaction status, storing the precise moment it occurred.
The audit trail is invisible to regular users but provides a detailed history for administrators and compliance.
A tamper-evident sequence of events with timestamps is available for review, ensuring traceability.
All access attempts and transactional updates are recorded with time marks for later accountability.
